XOnlineSaveLogonState

Retrieves the current state of an Xbox Live logon, including the logged on players and services. The returned data can be saved, then later used in a call to XOnlineRetrieveLogonState to retrieve information about the logon state.

HRESULT XOnlineSaveLogonState(
    PXONLINE_LOGON_STATE pLogonState
);

Parameters

pLogonState
[out] Pointer to an XONLINE_LOGON_STATE structure that receives the current logon state.

Return Values

If the function is successful, the return value is S_OK.

If the title is not currently logged onto Xbox Live, the return value is XONLINE_E_NO_SESSION.

Remarks

The data returned by XOnlineSaveLogonState is designed to be saved or passed as opaque data, then later extracted with the XOnlineRetrieveLogonState function. For example, to persist logon information through a reboot, the game title calls XOnlineSaveLogonState before the reboot and places the returned XONLINE_LOGON_STATE somewhere in the LAUNCH_DATA before calling XLaunchNewImage. After the reboot, the game title extracts the XONLINE_LOGON_STATE from the launch data and calls XOnlineRetrieveLogonState to extract all the parameters needed to call XOnlineLogon again.

The title must have a valid, currently active Xbox Live logon session in order for XOnlineSaveLogonState to succeed. Do not close the logon task handle (returned by XOnlineLogon) before calling XOnlineSaveLogonState.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

Xbox Live Functions | XOnlineRetrieveLogonState, XONLINE_LOGON_STATE
